titles.text: A Phase 2, Open-label Study to Assess Copper and Molybdenum Balance in Participants with Wilson Disease Treated with ALXN1840
Copper and Molybdenum Balance in Participants with Wilson Disease Treated with ALXN1840

narrativeContentItems.name: Adverse Events: Definitions and Procedures for Recording, Evaluating, Follow-up, and Reporting
Contraceptive Guidance and Collection of Pregnancy Information

amendments.name: Amendment

code.decode: Inconsistency And/or Error In The Protocol

amendments.summary: The main reason for preparation of this amendment was to update procedures outlined in the Schedule of Activities, remove contradictory text on the reporting of serious adverse events, and add details of an interim analysis. Additional, minor changes are included in the table below.

code.decode: Inconsistency And/or Error In The Protocol

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Age requirement
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participants aged ≥18 at the time of signing the ICF.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Diagnosis of Wilson Disease
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Diagnosis of WD by Leipzig Criteria ≥ 4 documented by testing as outlined in the 2012 European Association for the Study of Liver WD Clinical Practice Guidelines (Ferenci, 2003; EASL, 2012) or by historical test results for WD including some or all of the following: • Presence of Kayser Fleischer rings, • Neurological symptoms, • Serum ceruloplasmin below reference range, • Coombs-negative hemolytic anemia, • Elevated liver or urinary copper, • Presence of mutations in the ATP7B gene, or • Other, as considered appropriate, may be used instead to confirm the diagnosis of WD.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Benefit from decoppering therapy
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participants who in the opinion of the referring Investigator may benefit from decoppering therapy.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Ability to comply with study procedures
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participants must be able to comply with all study-related procedures.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Ability to reside in CRU
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participants must be able to reside in the CRU for intensive metabolic monitoring of copper and molybdenum.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Willingness to discontinue chelator therapy
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participants willing to discontinue chelator therapy for approximately 4 days prior to initiation of ALXN1840 to allow a baseline assessment of copper balance.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Adequate venous access
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participants with adequate venous access to allow collection of required blood samples.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Ability to swallow tablets
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participants must be able to swallow intact ALXN1840 tablets.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Willingness to avoid certain minerals
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participants willing to avoid use of minerals containing copper, zinc, or molybdenum throughout the study duration.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Adherence to controlled diet
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participants willing to adhere to copper/molybdenum-controlled diet during inpatient periods and willing to comply with a low copper dietary requirement during the Outpatient Period.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Contraception requirements
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Contraceptive use by men or women should be consistent with local regulations regarding the methods of contraception for those participating in clinical studies. a. Male participants: • Male participants, if heterosexually active and with a female spouse or partner of childbearing potential or a pregnant or breastfeeding spouse or partner, must agree to use barrier contraception (male condom) for the duration of the study and for at least 3 months after the end of systemic exposure of the study intervention. Male participants must not donate sperm for at least 3 months after the end of systemic exposure of the study intervention. • Female spouses or partners of male participants who are of childbearing potential must use highly effective contraception as defined below and in Section 10.4, starting at least 1 menstrual cycle before (the male participant’s) first study intervention administration and continuing until at least 3 months after the end of their male partner’s systemic exposure to the study intervention. • Barrier contraception (male condom) is required even with documented medical assessment of surgical success of a vasectomy. For male participants who have had a vasectomy (with documented evidence of azoospermia if possible) and agree to use a male condom for the stated time period, no additional contraceptive method is required by their female partner. b. Female participants: • Female participants or female partners of male participants of childbearing potential (including breastfeeding females), if heterosexually active, must be willing to follow protocol-specified contraception guidance starting at least 1 menstrual cycle before first study intervention administration and continuing for at least 3 months after the end of systemic exposure of the study intervention. Female participants must not donate ova for at least 3 months after the end of systemic exposure of the study intervention. • Female participants who are documented as being of non-childbearing potential as defined in Section 10.4 are exempt from contraception requirements. • Highly effective contraceptive methods for female participants and female partners of male participants are described in Section 10.4.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Informed consent
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participants capable of giving signed informed consent, which includes compliance with the requirements and restrictions listed in the ICF and in this protocol.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Decompensated cirrhosis or high MELD score
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Decompensated cirrhosis or model for end-stage liver disease (MELD) score > 13.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Modified Nazer score
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Modified Nazer score > 7 (Dhawan, 2005).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Gastrointestinal bleed
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Clinically significant gastrointestinal bleed within past 3 months.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Elevated ALT
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Alanine aminotransferase (ALT) > 2 × upper limit of normal (ULN).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Marked neurological disease
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Marked neurological disease requiring assistance with self-care or activities of daily life.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Low hemoglobin
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Hemoglobin less than lower limit of the reference range for age and sex.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Active viral infections
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Active infection with hepatitis B virus (positive hepatitis B surface antigen) or C virus (participants with positive hepatitis C antibody result would require confirmation of active disease with a positive hepatitis C polymerase chain reaction test), or seropositivity for human immunodeficiency virus (HIV).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Significant medical or psychiatric disorders
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: History or presence of/significant history of or current cardiovascular, respiratory, hepatic, renal, gastrointestinal, endocrinological, hematological, or neurological disorders, or psychiatric disorder that in the opinion of the Investigator may constitute a risk when taking the study intervention; or may interfere with the interpretation of data.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Previous treatment with ALXN1840 or tetrathiomolybdate
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Previous treatment with ALXN1840 or other form of tetrathiomolybdate within 1 year prior to dosing.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Previous treatment with zinc
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Previous treatment with zinc within 21 days prior to Day 1.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Prior experimental or unapproved therapy
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: The use of an experimental or unapproved/unlicensed therapy at the same time or within 90 days or 5 half-lives, whichever is longer, prior to the Screening Visit.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Renal failure
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participants in renal failure, defined as in end-stage renal disease on dialysis (chronic kidney disease [CKD] stage 5) or creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min (Levey, 2006).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Pregnancy or breastfeeding
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Pregnant (or females who are planning to become pregnant) or breastfeeding females (women of childbearing potential must have a negative serum pregnancy test result at screening).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Known sensitivity to study intervention
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Known sensitivity to ALXN1840, ALXN1840 excipients (anhydrous dicalcium phosphate, anhydrous sodium carbonate), or any of the ingredients contained in ALXN1840 or related compounds.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Likely non-compliant or uncooperative
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: In the opinion of the Investigator, the participant and/or their legal guardian is likely to be non-compliant or uncooperative during the study.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: History of substance or alcohol abuse
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: History of illicit drug abuse, history of significant alcohol abuse within 1 year prior to the Screening Visit, or clinical evidence of substance and/or alcohol abuse within the 2 years before screening. Alcohol abuse is defined as regular weekly intake of more than 14 units (for both males and females), using the following National Health Service (NHS) alcohol tracker http://www.nhs.uk/Tools/Pages/drinks-tracker.aspx.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Positive urine drug toxicology screen
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Positive urine drug toxicology screen at Screening or on Day -8 (cannabinoids will not be tested).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Alcohol consumption or positive breath test
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Alcohol consumption within 48 hours prior to study intervention administration or positive alcohol breath test at screening or on Day -8.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Unwillingness to complete meals or tolerate diet
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participants unwilling to consistently complete every meal and tolerate a controlled, limited menu for the duration of the study.

medicalDevices.name: Cannula
medicalDevices.label: Cannula
medicalDevices.description: Device that may be placed to minimize needle sticks for repeated blood draws.

medicalDevices.name: Catheter
medicalDevices.label: Catheter
medicalDevices.description: Device that may be placed for venous access, not to be left in place for longer than 72 hours.

medicalDevices.name: Automated Blood Pressure and Heart Rate Device
medicalDevices.label: Automated BP/HR Device
medicalDevices.description: Device used for consistent and reproducible assessment of blood pressure and heart rate.

medicalDevices.name: Water Bottle Dispenser
medicalDevices.label: Water Dispenser
medicalDevices.description: Large dispenser from which participants drink water for controlled fluid intake.

medicalDevices.name: SMS Text Messaging System
medicalDevices.label: SMS System
medicalDevices.description: System used by participants to confirm study intervention administration during the outpatient period.

medicalDevices.name: Male Condom
medicalDevices.label: Condom
medicalDevices.description: Barrier contraception method required for male participants who are heterosexually active with a female partner of childbearing potential or a pregnant/breastfeeding partner.

biomedicalConcepts.label: Pharmacodynamic Biomarker Measurements (Plasma Total and PUF Copper, LBC, Ceruloplasmin, Ceruloplasmin-bound Coppe

biomedicalConcepts.name: Retained Serum Sample
biomedicalConcepts.label: Collection of Retained Serum Sample for Safety Analysis

biomedicalConcepts.name: Anthropometric Measurements
biomedicalConcepts.label: Height, Weight, and Body Mass Index Measurements

biomedicalConcepts.name: Concomitant Medications and Therapies
biomedicalConcepts.label: Collection of Concomitant Medication and Non-Pharmacologic Therapy/Procedure Information

biomedicalConcepts.name: 24-Hour Urine Collection
biomedicalConcepts.label: 24-Hour Urine Collection for Copper and Molybdenum

biomedicalConcepts.name: Infectious Disease Screen
biomedicalConcepts.label: HIV, Hepatitis B, and Hepatitis C Screening

biomedicalConcepts.name: Fecal Sample Collection
biomedicalConcepts.label: Fecal Sample Collection for Copper and Molybdenum Analysis

biomedicalConcepts.name: Elimination and Menstruation Monitoring
biomedicalConcepts.label: Monitoring of Urination, Bowel Movements, and Menstruation

arms.name: ALXN1840 Treatment
arms.description: All participants receive ALXN1840 with dose titration from 15 mg/day to 30 mg/day.

objectives.name: Primary Objective: Assess net copper balance
objectives.text: Assess net copper balance with daily repeat-dose ALXN1840 treatment (15 mg and 30 mg) in participants with Wilson disease (WD)

endpoints.name: Primary Endpoint: Mean daily copper balance
endpoints.text: Mean daily copper balance where copper balance is measured by the calculated difference between copper intake (in food and drink) and copper output (in feces and urine) during ALXN1840 accumulation and steady-state periods for each dose

objectives.name: Secondary Objective: Assess change in copper balance
objectives.text: Assess change in copper balance in response to ALXN1840 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day during ALXN1840 accumulation and at steady state periods versus the pretreatment baseline in participants with WD

endpoints.name: Secondary Endpoint: Change in mean daily copper balance
endpoints.text: Change in mean daily copper balance as measured by the calculated difference between copper intake (in food and drink) and copper output (in feces and urine) from pretreatment baseline (Days -4 through -1) and ALXN1840 accumulation and steady-state periods for each dose

endpoints.name: Secondary Endpoint: Clinical laboratory assessments
endpoints.text: Clinical laboratory assessments (serum chemistry, hematology, coagulation, and urinalysis)

objectives.name: Secondary Objective: Investigate effect on copper disposition
objectives.text: Investigate the effect of ALXN1840 (15 mg/day and 30 mg/day) on the disposition of copper in participants with WD

endpoints.name: Secondary Endpoint: Copper quantified in food, drink, feces, urine, plasma total and LBC
endpoints.text: Copper quantified in food, drink, feces, and urine, including plasma total and labile bound copper (LBC) during ALXN1840 accumulation and steady-state periods for each dose

objectives.name: Secondary Objective: Assess steady-state total molybdenum balance
objectives.text: Assess steady-state total molybdenum balance as a measure of ALXN1840 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day in participants with WD

endpoints.name: Secondary Endpoint: Mean daily molybdenum balance
endpoints.text: Mean daily molybdenum balance as demonstrated through measurement of molybdenum intake (in food, drink and ALXN1840), and molybdenum output (feces and urine) representing ALXN1840 steady state

endpoints.name: Secondary Endpoint: Heart rate, intervals (PR, QRS, QT and QTc), and clinically significant electrocardiogram (ECG) findings
endpoints.text: Heart rate, intervals (PR, QRS, QT and QTc), and clinically significant electrocardiogram (ECG) findings as determined by triplicate 12-lead ECG

objectives.name: Secondary Objective: Assess accumulation of molybdenum
objectives.text: Assess accumulation of molybdenum with ALXN1840 treatment at 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day in participants with WD

endpoints.name: Secondary Endpoint: Accumulation of molybdenum
endpoints.text: Accumulation of molybdenum as determined by molybdenum balance

endpoints.name: Secondary Endpoint: Vital sign assessments
endpoints.text: Vital sign assessments (blood pressure and heart rate)

objectives.name: Secondary Objective: Determine steady-state plasma PK of molybdenum
objectives.text: Determine the steady-state plasma pharmacokinetic (PK) of total molybdenum and plasma ultrafiltrate (PUF) molybdenum (as surrogate measures of ALXN1840, 15 mg and 30 mg) in participants with WD

endpoints.name: Secondary Endpoint: PK parameters for plasma total and PUF-molybdenum
endpoints.text: PK parameters for plasma total and PUF-molybdenum

objectives.name: Secondary Objective: Evaluate safety and tolerability
objectives.text: Evaluate the safety and tolerability of repeated-dose administration of ALXN1840 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day in participants with WD

endpoints.name: Secondary Endpoint: Treatment emergent adverse events (TEAEs)/serious adverse events (SAE
endpoints.text: Treatment emergent adverse events (TEAEs)/serious adverse events (SAEs)

objectives.name: Exploratory Objective: Determine dose response for copper balance
objectives.text: Determine dose response of ALXN1840 15 mg/day and 30 mg/day for copper balance in participants with WD

endpoints.name: Exploratory Endpoint: Assess dose response of ALXN1840 on copper balance
endpoints.text: Assess dose response of ALXN1840 on copper balance focusing on copper balance

objectives.name: Exploratory Objective: Determine effect of treatment duration on copper balance
objectives.text: Determine the effect of treatment duration on copper balance in participants with WD

endpoints.name: Exploratory Endpoint: Determine the effect of time following initiation of ALXN1840 treatment on copper balance
endpoints.text: Determine the effect of time following initiation of ALXN1840 treatment on copper balance

objectives.name: Exploratory Objective: Assess effects on ceruloplasmin, ceruloplasmin-bound copper LBC profiles
objectives.text: Assess the effects of ALXN1840 on ceruloplasmin, ceruloplasmin-bound copper LBC profiles in plasma in participants with WD

endpoints.name: Exploratory Endpoint: Ceruloplasmin, ceruloplasmin-bound copper, and LBC: Change in ceruloplasmin
endpoints.text: Ceruloplasmin, ceruloplasmin-bound copper, and LBC: Change in ceruloplasmin at Days 1, 8, 25, 29, 36, and Day 39 compared with predose

estimands.name: Primary Efficacy Estimand: Mean Daily Copper Balance
estimands.populationSummary: Adult patients with Wilson Disease meeting eligibility criteria (Intent-to-Treat (ITT) Population) Summary measure: Mean.

intercurrentEvents.name: Treatment discontinuation
intercurrentEvents.text: Subject discontinues study treatment prior to the end of the scheduled treatment period.

intercurrentEvents.name: Dose modification or non-escalation
intercurrentEvents.text: Subject's dose is modified (decreased or not escalated to 30 mg/day) due to safety concerns or other reasons, as per protocol-defined dose adjustment criteria.

intercurrentEvents.name: Major protocol deviation impacting copper balance measurements
intercurrentEvents.text: Subject has a major protocol deviation that impacts the reliability of copper balance measurements (e.g., significant non-compliance with copper-controlled diet).

activities.name: HIV, hepatitis B and C screen
Blood sampling for PK: Plasma total Mo and PUF-Mo
PD: Plasma total and PUF-Cu, LBC, ceruloplasmin, ceruloplasmin-bound Cu
Urination and bowel movement monitoring, menstruation checks

plannedDuration.reasonDurationWillVary: Duration depends on individual subject progression and follow-up.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Ability to comply with study procedures
Decompensated cirrhosis or high MELD score
Previous treatment with ALXN1840 or tetrathiomolybdate
History of substance or alcohol abuse
Alcohol consumption or positive breath test
Unwillingness to complete meals or tolerate diet

notes.text: Abbreviations: ALT = alanine aminotransferase; AST = aspartate aminotransferase; IB = Investigator’s Brochure; PK = pharmacokinetics; WD = Wilson disease.
Abbreviations: CRF = case report form; CRU = clinical research unit; EOS = End of Study; SoA = Schedule of Activities.

studyInterventions.name: ALXN
studyInterventions.description: A novel copper binding agent in development for the treatment of Wilson disease.

studyInterventions.name: Copper- and Molybdenum-controlled diet
studyInterventions.description: A standardized diet provided during inpatient periods to control copper and molybdenum intake for balance measurements.

studyInterventions.name: Paracetamol/acetaminophen
studyInterventions.description: Permitted for use as an exception with Investigator approval, maximum 1000 mg per day.

studyInterventions.name: Ibuprofen
studyInterventions.description: Permitted for use with Investigator approval, dose not to exceed 1200 mg in any 24-hour period, with caution due to CYP2C9 substrate interaction.

studyInterventions.name: Penicillamine
studyInterventions.description: Copper-chelating therapy that must be discontinued starting on Day -4 prior to ALXN1840 treatment.

studyInterventions.name: Trientine
studyInterventions.description: Copper-chelating therapy that must be discontinued starting on Day -4 prior to ALXN1840 treatment.

studyInterventions.name: Zinc therapy
studyInterventions.description: Must be discontinued for a minimum of 21 days prior to Day 1.

studyInterventions.name: Prescription medications (genera
studyInterventions.description: Prohibited within 14 days or 5 half-lives (whichever is longer) of Day -7.

studyInterventions.name: Nonprescription drugs (including vitamins and dietary or herbal supplement
studyInterventions.description: Prohibited within 7 days or 5 half-lives (whichever is longer) before Day -7.

studyInterventions.name: Herbal remedies
studyInterventions.description: Prohibited; must be discontinued at least 7 days prior to first admission until study completion.

studyInterventions.name: St John’s wort
studyInterventions.description: Prohibited; must be discontinued 2 weeks before the planned first study intervention administration.

studyInterventions.name: Minerals containing copper, zinc, or molybdenum
